statement:
# Maximize Stacked Boxes
You have n boxes; box i has width w_i, depth d_i, height h_i. Box i can be stacked on box j only if, after optionally rotating box i (any of its 3 dimensions may be chosen as height, the other two as the base), i's base strictly fits inside j's base (both base dimensions strictly smaller). Each box may be used at most once. Build a single vertical stack maximizing total height.
Input: first line n (1<=n<=2000). Next n lines: w_i d_i h_i (1<=each<=10^9). Output: the maximum achievable total height.

# Minimum Knights Dominating Set on a Board
Given an R x C board (1<=R,C<=12) with some cells blocked, place the minimum number of knights on non-blocked cells so that every non-blocked cell is either occupied by a knight or attacked by at least one knight (standard chess knight moves). Knights do not block each other.
Input: R C, then R lines of C characters ('.'=free, '#'=blocked). Output: the minimum number of knights, or -1 if impossible.

# Convoy Scheduling to Minimize Makespan
n jobs must each be processed first on machine A then on machine B (flow shop, 2 machines). Job i takes a_i time on A and b_i on B. A processes one job at a time; likewise B; a job can start on B only after finishing on A. Choose the processing order to minimize the completion time of the last job on B.
Input: n (1<=n<=2*10^5), then n lines a_i b_i (1<=a_i,b_i<=10^9). Output: the minimum makespan.

# Online Kth Nearest in 1D with Weights
There are q operations on a multiset of integer points, initially empty. Operation types: '1 x' insert point x; '2 x' remove one occurrence of point x (guaranteed present); '3 x k' output the k-th smallest value of |p - x| over all points p currently in the set (guaranteed at least k points). Ties by value count with multiplicity.
Input: q (1<=q<=2*10^5), then q lines. |x|<=10^9, 1<=k<=size. Output one line per type-3 query.

# Minimum Strokes to Paint a Grid
An R x C grid must be painted to a target pattern of colors 1..K. Initially blank. In one stroke you pick a color and a full row OR a full column and paint every cell in it that color (overwriting). Find the minimum number of strokes to reach exactly the target, or report impossible.
Input: R C K (1<=R,C<=50, 1<=K<=10), then R lines of C integers (target). Output: minimum strokes or -1.

# Balanced Tree Edge Removal
Given a tree with n nodes, remove exactly one edge to split it into two components; the cost is |size1 - size2| times the maximum edge weight on the path... no: cost = |size1 - size2|. Then, independently, you may also relocate at most one leaf to any other node. Minimize the achievable |size1 - size2| after the single edge removal (relocation applies before removal). 
Input: n (2<=n<=2*10^5), then n-1 lines u v. Output: minimum possible |size1 - size2|.

# Largest Sum-Free Subset (Heuristic Scoring)
Given a multiset S of n positive integers, find a large subset T of S that is sum-free: no three (not necessarily distinct indices, but distinct chosen elements) a,b,c in T satisfy a+b=c. Output any valid sum-free subset; you are scored by |T|. 
Input: n (1<=n<=100000), then the n integers (1<=v<=10^9). Output: first line |T|, second line the chosen elements. Must be a valid sum-free subset.

# Cover a String with Fewest Palindromic Substrings
Partition a string s (lowercase, |s|<=3000) into the minimum number of contiguous substrings each of which is a palindrome. Output that minimum count.
Input: s. Output: the minimum number of palindromic pieces.

# Minimum Cost to Make Flow Feasible
A directed graph has n nodes, m edges; edge e has capacity cap_e and per-unit cost cost_e. You must send exactly F units from node 1 to node n. Additionally you may increase any edge's capacity by paying add_e per extra unit of capacity added to that edge. Minimize (flow cost) + (capacity-augmentation cost) to feasibly route F units.
Input: n m F, then m lines u v cap cost add. (2<=n<=400, 1<=m<=3000, 1<=F<=10^6). Output: the minimum total cost, or -1 if impossible even with augmentation... (augmentation is unbounded so always feasible if a path exists).

# API: AdaptiveSpotScheduler (CPU-only)
Implement a class `AdaptiveSpotScheduler` with methods:
  __init__(self, deadline: int, work: int, restart_overhead: int, spot_price: float, ondemand_price: float)
  decide(self, t: int, spot_available: bool, progress: float) -> str   # returns 'spot' or 'ondemand'
A job needs `work` units of progress by time `deadline`. Each timestep advances progress by 1 if you run (on either resource) and the resource is available; choosing 'spot' when spot becomes unavailable that step wastes it and incurs `restart_overhead` lost progress. Minimize expected cost while guaranteeing completion by the deadline. You are scored by total cost across a hidden sequence of availability. Use an efficient online policy; do not assume future availability.

# API: SegmentedCache (CPU-only)
Implement class `SegmentedCache` with __init__(self, capacity: int) and get(self, key:int)->int (returns value or -1) and put(self, key:int, value:int)->None. Beyond LRU, maintain a frequency-aware segmented policy (probationary + protected segments) to maximize hit rate on skewed access streams. Must be O(1) amortized per op with capacity up to 10^6. Scored by hit rate on hidden traces.

# API: Triton row-softmax kernel (GPU L4)
Implement `def softmax(x)` taking a 2D torch.cuda.FloatTensor of shape (M, N) and returning row-wise softmax as a torch.cuda tensor, using a Triton kernel (triton 3.2.0). Must be numerically stable (subtract row max) and handle N up to 8192 with one program per row. Scored by correctness (max abs error vs torch.softmax < 1e-4) and throughput on an L4.
